verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 encodes a type I integral membrane protein (TGN46/TGN51) that is a well-characterised resident of the trans-Golgi network. Its canonical localization is the TGN lumenal face, and it cycles between early endosomes and the TGN — both endomembrane compartments. Its ectodomain faces the TGN lumen / endosomal lumen, not the extracellular space. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets TGOLN2 on the outer leaflet. (2) Not a sheddase substrate with a documented PM precursor stage. (3) No stable covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the cell surface. (4) No documented minority PM pool; cycling is between TGN and endosomes, not the plasma membrane. (5) No cancer/cell-death/germline/activation-induced outer-leaflet evidence for this protein specifically. No CD designation or surface-family lineage. Verdict: endomembrane-resident.
